_comment: Stylized summary of WWF Terrestrial Ecoregions of the World, keyed to Eco's 12 biome layers. Each biome_vector is a proportion (0..1) reflecting that region's approximate biome mix. Source: WWF 'Terrestrial Ecoregions of the World' (Olson et al. 2001) — proportions are hand-estimated from the Olson biome map, not API-extracted. Committed once so the MCP tool has no runtime CDN dependency (claude-ai-mcp#40 CSP).

regions.name: Boreal Shield (Canadian taiga)
regions.description: Conifer-dominated taiga with countless glacial lakes and peat-bog wetlands. Long frozen winters, short cool summers.

regions.name: Pacific Northwest temperate rainforest
regions.description: Moss-draped coastal rainforest on a mountainous fjord coast. Huge conifers, salmon rivers, abundant rainfall.

regions.name: Mediterranean basin
regions.description: Hot dry summers, mild wet winters. Scrubby evergreen oak and pine forest transitioning into xeric shrubland and farmland.

regions.name: Amazon rainforest basin
regions.description: Closed-canopy equatorial rainforest threaded with blackwater rivers and seasonal floodplains. Highest terrestrial biodiversity on Earth.

regions.name: Sahara desert belt
regions.description: The planet's largest hot desert. Bare rock, sand seas, and sparse xerophytic scrub, fringed by a Sahel of dry grassland.

regions.name: Great Plains temperate grassland
regions.description: Continental mid-latitude grassland — tallgrass prairie in the east grading to shortgrass steppe in the rain-shadowed west.

regions.name: Arctic tundra
regions.description: Treeless permafrost plain north of the boreal tree line. Short growing season, lichens and dwarf shrubs, winter pack ice offshore.

regions.name: Indo-Pacific archipelago
regions.description: Oceanic tropical region — volcanic islands of rainforest and mangrove swamp scattered across warm continental-shelf seas.

regions.name: Appalachian temperate forest
regions.description: Deciduous broadleaf forest on worn-down ancient mountains. Hot-humid summers, snowy winters, rich understory biodiversity.

regions.name: Antarctic ice sheet
regions.description: Continental polar desert of glacial ice, surrounded by the Southern Ocean. Essentially no vegetated land biomes.

regions.name: Everglades subtropical wetland
regions.description: A slow-moving river of grass threading mangrove swamp and cypress forest at the tip of a subtropical peninsula.

regions.name: Patagonian coastal steppe
regions.description: Cold dry grassland in the Andean rain shadow, meeting fjord rainforest to the west and the South Atlantic to the east.
